commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh I have caused you to multiply as the bud of the field, and you have increased and become great, and you are come to excellent beauty: your breasts were formed, and your hair had grown, yet you were naked and bare. Hebrew, "the ornament of ornaments "hadaiim instead of harim in Septuagint, "the cities of cities "(Calmet) or the highest glory, being arrived at that age when decorations are most sought after. Literally, "swelling. "Septuagint, "erect. "(Haydock) Hair, (pilus. ) Women are allowed by the canon law to marry at twelve.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
